pi_items.verified_value: RNLI Severn Class is a real all-weather lifeboat introduced in 1996, 17.3m long, 42 tonnes, capable of 25 knots with 250nm range
pi_items.source: RNLI official documentation and Wikipedia articles on Severn-class lifeboat
GPS coordinates from latitude.to and official geographic data; Lizard Point coordinates
GPS coordinates from latitude.to; Lizard Point is the southernmost point on mainland Great Britain

pi_items.verified_value: Falmouth is a real port city in Cornwall, UK, home to MRCC Falmouth (Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre)
pi_items.source: HM Coastguard official locations and Falmouth Coastguard documentation

pi_items.verified_value: 8.0 corresponds to Beaufort Force 8 (Gale): winds 34-40 knots, moderately high waves, storm conditions
pi_items.source: NOAA Beaufort Wind Scale, Royal Meteorological Society, Weather.gov

pi_items.verified_value: VHF Channel 16 is the international maritime distress and calling channel (156.8 MHz); Channel 67 is a navigational channel for ship-to-ship messages
pi_items.source: USCG Navigation Center - International VHF Marine Radio Channels and Frequencies

pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is an open-source maritime simulator released under GPL v2, available at https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc. Built with C++ and Irrlicht 3D library, used for training in navigation, radar, and seamanship
pi_items.source: Bridge Command GitHub repository and official website (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/)

pi_items.metadata_value: Lizard SAR Exercise Storm based on Lizard Point, Cornwall
pi_items.verified_value: Lizard Point (49.9553, -5.2038) is a real geographic location at the southern tip of the Lizard Peninsula in Cornwall, UK; appropriate for a SAR training scenario given proximity to maritime rescue operations zone
pi_items.source: Geographic coordinate databases, Wikipedia articles on Lizard Point and Lizard Peninsula

pi_items.metadata_value: MRCC Falmouth mentioned in task description as context for Coast Guard operations officer role
pi_items.verified_value: MRCC Falmouth is a real Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre located at Pendennis in Falmouth, Cornwall, part of HM Coastguard. One of 10 MRCCs covering over 11,000 miles of UK coastline
pi_items.source: HM Coastguard official locations; Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre documentation

pi_items.metadata_value: Required vessel types: Fishing Vessel, Tanker, Container Ship, Patrol, Yacht, Cargo
pi_items.verified_value: All vessel types are standard maritime vessel classifications used in shipping, commercial operations, and rescue scenarios. Generic but realistic vessel types suitable for a maritime training simulator
pi_items.source: Maritime industry standards; Bridge Command simulator capabilities

privileged_info_summary: The storm_sar_scenario_creation task is a synthetic scenario creation exercise using the open-source Bridge Command maritime simulator (GPL v2, https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc). The scenario is based on realistic references: Lizard Point, Cornwall (49.9553°N, -5.2038°W) is the real southernmost point on mainland Great Britain; RNLI Severn Class is a documented all-weather lifeboat class (17.3m, 42 tonnes, 25 knots); MRCC Falmouth is the real Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre in Cornwall; Beaufort Force 8 (weather=8.0) represents gale conditions with 34-40 knot winds; VHF Channel 16 is the international maritime distress channel (156.8 MHz). The task requires participants to create supporting documentation (SAR briefing) using realistic maritime terminology and communication protocols. All geographic, vessel, and operational references are based on verified real-world maritime systems and locations.
